title: Watchlist — 2023–2025 evolving cinematic taste
html:
TL;DR

  • A long-running watchlist created Jul 2023, modified through Apr 2025 — Yahir's evolving cinematic taste over ~22 months.
  • Heavy auteur lean: Scorsese (Raging Bull), Miyazaki (Howl's Moving Castle), Wes Anderson (Grand Budapest), Stanley Kubrick adjacents, Linklater (Dazed and Confused), Fellini's spirit (Donnie Darko, Magnolia).
  • Cult / weird-cinema strain: The Greasy Strangler, Vivarium, Gummo, Talk to Me, Tulsa King, Love Death + Robots — pointing to a weird cinema affinity preference.
  • Sci-fi / dystopia bench: Dark City (twice), Interstellar, Tree of Life, 28 Days Later, Shogun, The Wild Robot.
  • Crowd-pleasers tucked alongside the weird stuff: School of Rock, Forrest-Gump-coded Dirty Dancing, Flashdance, White Men Can't Jump.
  • Watchlist as taste-self-portrait: pairs with the shared 2025 partner watchlist for the diff.

Key picks

Donnie Darko, Magnolia, Raging Bull, Howl's Moving Castle, Tree of Life, Vivarium, Gummo, Lost in Translation, The Greasy Strangler

Why it matters in the graph

  • A clean single-source data set for Yahir's solo film taste — useful baseline against the 2025 shared-watchlist for diffing solo vs partnered taste.
  • The Magnolia / Tree of Life / Lost in Translation / Virgin Suicides cluster signals an introspective-melancholic-meditative strain that aligns with the normal neutral to everything concept and Yahir's late-night journaling notes.
  • The 28 Days Later + Vivarium pairing inside the post-COVID-era watchlist is itself an artifact of covid pandemic era — pandemic horror chosen during pandemic drift.

Open questions

  • Which titles actually got watched vs only listed?
  • Pair with 2024 09 26 top anime hype scene to triangulate his anime-vs-live-action taste split.
